violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: 'echo "$stdin_json" | python3 "$HOOK" >/dev/null 2>"$WORK/stderr.log"'
violations.recommendation: Activate venv before invoking python hooks: 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && echo ... | python "$HOOK"'

violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: 'echo "{...}" | python3 "$HOOK" >/dev/null 2>"$STDERR_A_LOG"'
violations.recommendation: Activate venv before invoking python hooks: 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && echo ... | python "$HOOK"'

violations.finding: Direct python3 invocation without venv activation: 'python3 - <<PYEOF' inline heredoc execution
violations.recommendation: Extract inline python to a named script and invoke via venv: 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/generate-test-transcript.py'

violations.finding: Hardcoded path literal '/root/.claude/docs/dev/' with no parameter override; CONTROL_ROOT variable exists but is not applied to the .claude-prefixed candidate
violations.recommendation: Replace hardcoded candidate with: "${CLAUDE_ROOT:-/root/.claude}/docs/dev/close-report-${TASK_ID}.md" where CLAUDE_ROOT is an added configurable parameter

violations.finding: HOOK variable hardcoded to '/root/.claude/hooks/stop-spec-coverage-enforce.py' with no parameter fallback
violations.recommendation: Use: HOOK="${HOOK:-/root/.claude/hooks/stop-spec-coverage-enforce.py}" to allow override in different environments

violations.finding: Same as no-hardcoded-values finding: hardcoded /root/.claude/docs/dev/ path has no environment variable or CLI parameter override
violations.recommendation: Add CLAUDE_ROOT parameter with default: CLAUDE_ROOT="${CLAUDE_ROOT:-/root/.claude}" and reference it for the third candidate path

violations.finding: HOOK path not parameterized; test harness cannot be pointed at an alternative hook installation without editing the file

standards_passed: no-inline-code (Standard 1) — N/A: no .md files in changed set
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4) — no decimal or lettered step numbering found
meaningful-naming (Standard 5) — no forbidden naming patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, *-v2, etc.)
english-only (Standard 6) — no CJK/Chinese characters found in in-scope files (non-ASCII box-drawing chars in test-lock-detection.sh are not CJK)
merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7) — no obvious duplicate functionality among changed scripts
concise-documentation (Standard 8) — N/A: no .md files in changed set
dangling-references (Standard 10) — hooks/stop-spec-coverage-enforce.py referenced at tests/verify-stop-spec-session-isolation.sh:9 exists on disk
merge-conflicts (Standard 11) — no conflict markers (<<<<<<</=======/>>>>>>>) found in any changed file

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 3 issues: (1) Standard 9 listed as 'confirmed passing' while also saying 'same findings as Standard 2' — inconsistency requiring explicit Standard 9 violations; (2) all violations are pre-existing/outside changed hunks, recommended blocks_current_diff field; (3) Standard 6 PASS wording said 'no non-ASCII characters' which is factually wrong (box-drawing chars exist) vs the actual CJK-scoped rule. Two OBSERVATION_ONLY items confirmed draft Standard 3 classifications are correct.

findings.issue: Standard 9 listed as 'confirmed passing' while draft simultaneously says 'Same findings as Standard 2' — logically inconsistent; Standard 9 violations should be explicitly listed for resolve-close-report.sh:22 and verify-stop-spec-session-isolation.sh:9
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit parameterized-scripts violations for scripts/resolve-close-report.sh:22 and tests/verify-stop-spec-session-isolation.sh:9
findings.rationale: Draft was internally inconsistent; codex correctly identified the contradiction. Explicit Standard 9 violations added to the violations array.

findings.issue: All 5 draft violations are pre-existing / outside changed hunks; recommended adding blocks_current_diff: false field
findings.proposed_fix: Add blocks_current_diff: false to each pre-existing violation
findings.rationale: Helpful for downstream close.md diff-overlap check; applied to all violations.

findings.issue: Standard 6 PASS wording 'no non-ASCII characters found' is factually inaccurate — test-lock-detection.sh contains box-drawing and check-mark characters. Standard 6 targets CJK/Chinese characters only.
findings.proposed_fix: Change wording to: 'english-only (Standard 6) — no CJK/Chinese characters found in in-scope files (non-ASCII box-drawing chars in test-lock-detection.sh are not CJK)'
findings.rationale: Factual inaccuracy in PASS description corrected.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Draft Standard 3 findings at lines 65, 105, and 210 are not false positives under the written rule; introduced_in_diff: false classification is correct
findings.rationale: Confirmation that Standard 3 findings are correctly identified.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: resolve-close-report.sh:22 and verify-stop-spec-session-isolation.sh:9 are genuine parameterization concerns if full-file style auditing is allowed; proposed fixes are directionally correct
findings.rationale: Confirmation that Standard 2/9 findings are genuine.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied all 3 blockers/major findings: (1) added explicit Standard 9 violations for resolve-close-report.sh:22 and verify-stop-spec-session-isolation.sh:9; (2) added blocks_current_diff: false to all pre-existing violations; (3) corrected Standard 6 PASS wording from 'no non-ASCII' to 'no CJK/Chinese characters'.
